```mermaid
graph TB

        GF[git-filter]

        XTASK[xtask]
        DOCS[docs/]
        HOOKS[hooks/]

    end

        LEFTHOOK[lefthook-rs]
        GIT[Git Operations]

    ORCH --> GF

    GF --> MOD

    XTASK --> DOCS

    GF --> GIT

    HB --> WASM
    GF --> WASM
    WR --> WASM

    CONFIG --> XTASK
    SCHEMAS --> VH
    TEMPLATES --> XTASK
    SCRIPTS --> XTASK

    %% Data Flow
    GIT -.-> GF
    GF -.-> VH
    VH -.-> HB
    HB -.-> WASM
```

## File Type Policy Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    START[File Creation/Modification] --> CHECK{File Type Check}
    
    CHECK -->|.rs or .jsonc| ALLOWED[✅ Allowed Source File]
    CHECK -->|Other Extensions| GENERATED{Generated File?}
    
    GENERATED -->|Yes, with marker| VALID[✅ Valid Generated File]
    GENERATED -->|No marker| VIOLATION[❌ Policy Violation]
    
    ALLOWED --> VALIDATE[File Validation]
    VALID --> VALIDATE
    VIOLATION --> FIX[Add Generated Marker]
    FIX --> VALIDATE
    
    VALIDATE --> PASS[✅ Policy Compliant]
    VALIDATE --> FAIL[❌ Validation Failed]
    
    PASS --> COMMIT[Allow Commit]
    FAIL --> REJECT[Reject Commit]
```

## Data Flow Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant CLI as CLI Core
    participant Orch as Orchestrator
    participant GF as Git Filter
    participant VH as Validation Handler
    participant HB as Hook Builder
    participant WR as Worktree Runner
    participant Git as Git Repository

    User->>CLI: Execute Command
    CLI->>Orch: Route Command
    Orch->>GF: Process Git Operations
    GF->>Git: Read Repository State
    Git-->>GF: Return Git Data
    GF->>VH: Validate Contracts
    VH-->>GF: Validation Results
    GF->>HB: Build Hooks
    HB-->>GF: Hook Code
    GF->>WR: Execute in Worktree
    WR->>Git: Apply Changes
    Git-->>WR: Operation Results
    WR-->>GF: Execution Results
    GF-->>Orch: Processing Complete
    Orch-->>CLI: Command Results
    CLI-->>User: Display Results
```

## WebAssembly Integration

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "Rust Components"
        CLI[cli-core]
        GF[git-filter]
        HB[hook-builder]
        WR[worktree-runner]
    end

    subgraph "WIT Interfaces"
        WIT_CLI[cli.wit]
        WIT_GF[git-filter.wit]
        WIT_HB[hook-builder.wit]
        WIT_WR[worktree-runner.wit]
    end

    subgraph "WebAssembly Runtime"
        WASM[WASM Runtime]
        COMPONENTS[WASM Components]
    end

    CLI -.-> WIT_CLI
    GF -.-> WIT_GF
    HB -.-> WIT_HB
    WR -.-> WIT_WR

    WIT_CLI --> WASM
    WIT_GF --> WASM
    WIT_HB --> WASM
    WIT_WR --> WASM

    WASM --> COMPONENTS
```

## Build Pipeline

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    START[Source Code] --> VALIDATE[File Type Validation]
    VALIDATE --> GEN[Code Generation]
    GEN --> BUILD[Component Build]
    BUILD --> TEST[Testing]
    TEST --> DOCS[Documentation Generation]
    DOCS --> SCHEMA[Schema Validation]
    SCHEMA --> CONTRACT[Contract Validation]
    CONTRACT --> INTEGRATION[Integration Tests]
    INTEGRATION --> DEPLOY[Deployment Ready]
```

## Key Architectural Principles

### 1. Modular Design
- **Clear separation** of concerns across components
- **Minimal dependencies** between components
- **WebAssembly interfaces** for cross-language support

### 2. File Type Policy
- **Source files** (`.rs`, `.jsonc`) are manually maintained
- **Generated files** must have appropriate markers
- **Validation** ensures compliance with policy

### 3. Component Communication
- **Orchestrator pattern** for component coordination
- **Event-driven architecture** for loose coupling
- **Contract-based validation** for data integrity

### 4. Build Automation
- **xtask-driven** build process
- **Code generation** for repetitive tasks
- **Validation** at multiple stages

### 5. Git Integration
- **Lefthook** for hook management
- **Git filters** for content processing
- **Worktree management** for isolated operations

This architecture provides a robust, maintainable, and extensible foundation for git hook automation and development tooling.
